fields.description:
## Context

When related epics close, their deliverables may change the codebase in ways that affect in-progress implementation plans. Sprint drift detection currently only checks git history against file impact tables — it doesn't follow relates_to links to detect when a coordinating epic's closure introduces new capabilities or changes assumptions. This caused epics 8df3-61d0 and 07cb-5477 to ship independently without coordinating their outputs, despite addressing the same test quality problem space.

## Success Criteria

- sprint-drift-check.sh follows relates_to links on the current epic: when a related epic transitioned to closed after the implementation plan was created, emit REPLAN_TRIGGER with the closed epic ID and a summary of what changed — using the same contract as git-history drift detection
- Drift detection is one-hop only: checks direct relates_to neighbors, not neighbors-of-neighbors
- Brainstorm's Part C scan suggests relates_to links to open (not closed/archived) epics when cross-epic artifact overlap is discovered during gap analysis
- The relates_to link suggestion is presented to the user for approval before creating the link
- After deployment, if two epics are linked via relates_to and one closes, the next sprint drift check on the other epic's stories emits REPLAN_TRIGGER — verified by test scenario with two linked epics

## Scenario Analysis

- Empty relates_to set must exit cleanly (high): Guard clause for epics with no links
- Pre-existing closures must not trigger (high): Compare closure timestamp against implementation plan timestamp
- Ticket CLI failure on neighbor lookup (high): Wrap in error handling, warn and continue
- Missing implementation plan timestamp (medium): Fallback to story creation timestamp
- Part C suggests link to closed epic (medium): Filter by status=open at suggestion time
- Race condition between phases (low): Accept that mid-run closures caught on next run
- Declined link re-suggested (low): Known limitation, documented

## Dependencies

- fed2-b1ce — Part C scan provides cross-epic discovery (SC3-4 blocked until delivered)
- 1f67-e7b2 — relates_to bridge infrastructure (complementary, not blocking for SC1-2)

## Approach

Extend sprint-drift-check.sh with relates_to traversal using the ticket CLI. One-hop neighbor query, compare closure timestamps against implementation plan timestamp, emit REPLAN_TRIGGER per existing contract. Brainstorm Part C suggests relates_to links inline during gap analysis — user approves before creation. No new scripts; extends two existing mechanisms. Flag ALL related closures (no coupling-strength filtering).
